elements.html:
Dr Zachary Howard
School of Psychological Sciences
The University of Western Australia
35 Stirling Highway, Crawley WA 6009
Email: Zach.Howard@uwa.edu.au

Participant Information Form

Project title: Understanding the factors contributing to working memory demands

Researchers: Dr Zachary Howard (CI), Professor Shayne Loft

Student Researchers: Ms Yanyu Chen (PhD), Ms Nirvana Singh (Honours)

Invitation

You are invited to take part in this research project. This participant information form contains detailed information about the research project, and aims to explain to you as openly and clearly as possible all the procedures involved in this project before you decide whether or not to take part. Please read this information carefully, and feel free to ask questions about any information in the document.

Once you understand what the purpose of this project is and if you agree to take part in it, you will be asked to agree to a Statement of Informed Consent. By indicating your agreement with the statement of consent, you indicate that you understand the information provided to you and that you give your consent to participate in the research project.

Aim of the Study (What is the project about?)

The research aims to study human prospective memory. In our every-day lives we often need to remember to perform actions in the future (e.g., take medication, turn the oven off etc.), and this requires a cognitive function referred to as prospective memory. We are interested in learning more about the factors that influence an individual’s ability to remember to perform actions in the future.

Who can participate?

Because the study measures the abilities that change with age, participation is only open to anyone aged over 17 years. Due to task requirements we will also require participants to have normal or corrected-to-normal vision, and no self-reported hearing impairments.

What does participation involve?

The experiment will be conducted online, over 1 hour. Using your own computer, after reading the study information sheet and (if appropriate) agreeing to the consent information, you will be presented visual instructions for the two tasks.

One task requires you to view and remember a series of visual or auditory stimuli. These stimuli may be images, letters, digits, or words. You will respond using simple keyboard responses. The other task is called the Prospective Memory (PM) task. You will be instructed to remember to make a different response based on some criteria of the stimuli in the ongoing task. Each time the specific stimulus appears you will make a “PM response” instead of the usual response.

Voluntary Participation and Withdrawal from the Study

As you are voluntarily participating in this research, you are free to withdraw from the study at any time, and for any reason. If choosing to withdraw, you do not have to provide any explanation of your decision, but your data cannot be removed from the database as it is completely deidentified.

Your name will not be associated with any data file. Importantly, non-participation, withdrawal from the study, or withholding of data, will not affect your relationship with the University of Western Australia. For students of the University, withdrawal will have no effect on your grades.

Your privacy

Your performance data will not disclose any identifying information about you without your written permission or as required by law. However, de-identified data may be made openly accessible and that data can not be withdrawn (as referred to in the consent form).

All data from this study will be stored securely in accordance with regulations prescribed by the University of Western Australia and the National Health & Medical Research Council of Australia. Data will be deidentified and stored in a secure Pavlovia server, with copies stored in password protected UWA-managed research storage for 7 years.

Possible Benefits

There may not be direct benefit to you as a participant in this research. Data obtained from this research will be used to advance scientific knowledge concerning human prospective memory.

Possible Risks and Risk Management Plan

The research project is designed to investigate prospective memory. There are no foreseeable risks associated with this research.

Reimbursement

If you are a student enrolled in an undergraduate Psychology unit, you may be eligible to claim credit towards a research participation component of your unit. Participants who are not students or who are not enrolled in units where research participation can be credited will be offered $15 dollar per hour spent to cover incidental expenses such as travel and parking.

How can I access the results?

The results of the study will be published in scientific journals. We will also prepare a plain English summary of results which can be distributed on request (your contact details will not be stored but you may contact the researchers using the below details).

Contacts

The study has ethical approval from the UWA Human Research Ethics Committee. Please feel free to discuss your participation in this study with the following project staff, who can be contacted as follows:

Professor Shayne Loft
Phone: 6488 4610
Email: shayne.loft@uwa.edu.au
Dr Zachary Howard
Email: Zach.Howard@uwa.edu.au

Consent Statement

I have read the information provided and any questions I have asked have been answered to my satisfaction. I agree to participate in this research project but withdrawal is not possible once data are submitted.

I understand that all identifiable information that I provide is treated as confidential and will not be released by the investigator in any form that may identify me unless I have consented to this. The only exception to this principle of confidentiality is if this information is required by law to be released.
